commit.message:
fix: UnicodeEncodeError on Windows CP1252 terminals (box-drawing + emoji)

Windows CI failure: dashboard.py prints box-drawing char U+2500 and emoji
(globe, lock) which CP1252 cannot encode:
  UnicodeEncodeError: charmap codec can't encode character '\u2500'

Two fixes:
1. _run_server(): replace fragile TextIOWrapper re-wrap with stream.reconfigure(
   encoding='utf-8', errors='replace') — works reliably on Python 3.7+
2. cli.py: also reconfigure on entry for CLI-invoked paths
3. dashboard.py print: ASCII fallback chars on win32 (belt-and-suspenders)

Bumps to 0.12.12.

commit.message:
fix: ValueError on Windows Python 3.14 (argparse color detection)

Python 3.14 added color support to argparse which calls can_colorize()
-> file.fileno() on a closed file on Windows, raising ValueError:

  File clawmetry/cli.py, line 562, in main
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="cmd")
  ValueError: I/O operation on closed file

Fix: set NO_COLOR=1 on win32 before argparse initializes, which disables
the colorize code path in Python 3.14's argparse entirely.

Ref: https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/127321

commit.message:
chore: bump to v0.12.7 [RELEASE]

Includes fix: validate_key() accepts hostname and existing_node_id kwargs
clawmetry connect was broken with unexpected keyword argument hostname

fix: validate_key() accepts hostname and existing_node_id kwargs (#92)

* fix: move _build_gateway_data before __main__ block in sync.py

When sync.py is run as a script (systemd ExecStart=python3 sync.py),
Python executes the if __name__=="__main__" block at line 1588 BEFORE
processing line 1603 where _build_gateway_data is defined.

run_daemon() -> sync_system_snapshot() -> _build_gateway_data() would
always raise: NameError: name _build_gateway_data is not defined

Fix: move _build_gateway_data to line 1589, before the __main__ block.

Affects all users running the systemd/launchd service via pip install.

* fix: validate_key() accepts hostname and existing_node_id kwargs

cli.py calls validate_key(api_key, hostname=..., existing_node_id=...)
but sync.py only accepted api_key, causing:
  validate_key() got an unexpected keyword argument hostname

Fix: add hostname and existing_node_id as optional params, pass them
in the /auth payload when present.

verification.payload:
tree 49c952da240fac7b2b2e1a209253df2eb3362d29
parent 90fe0e2c6ecc5888a3e5cc1afc64869c50dcf098
author Vivek Chand <vivekchand19@gmail.com> 1773217752 +0100
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1773217752 +0100

fix: validate_key() accepts hostname and existing_node_id kwargs (#92)

* fix: move _build_gateway_data before __main__ block in sync.py

When sync.py is run as a script (systemd ExecStart=python3 sync.py),
Python executes the if __name__=="__main__" block at line 1588 BEFORE
processing line 1603 where _build_gateway_data is defined.

run_daemon() -> sync_system_snapshot() -> _build_gateway_data() would
always raise: NameError: name _build_gateway_data is not defined

Fix: move _build_gateway_data to line 1589, before the __main__ block.

Affects all users running the systemd/launchd service via pip install.

* fix: validate_key() accepts hostname and existing_node_id kwargs

cli.py calls validate_key(api_key, hostname=..., existing_node_id=...)
but sync.py only accepted api_key, causing:
  validate_key() got an unexpected keyword argument hostname

Fix: add hostname and existing_node_id as optional params, pass them
in the /auth payload when present.

commit.message:
fix: telegram channel panel — correct log grep patterns + sessions path (#88)

- Was grep-ing for 'messageChannel=telegram' and 'telegram.*deliver' which never appear in OpenClaw logs
- Actual outbound pattern: 'telegram sendMessage ok chat=... message=...' in field '1'
- Fixed sessions fallback path: ~/.clawdbot/ → ~/.openclaw/ (6 occurrences)

commit.message: fix: pass auth token to /api/flow-events SSE + bump to 0.12.2
chore: bump to v0.12.1

fix: flow bubbles — use /api/flow-events SSE instead of log pattern matching (#83)

* fix: flow bubbles — connect Flow tab to /api/flow-events SSE (not log-stream pattern matching)

Log patterns like 'run start'/'messagechannel'/'tool_use' never appear in actual OpenClaw logs.
The /api/flow-events SSE endpoint already exists and correctly tails both gateway.log and
session JSONL to emit typed {msg_in, msg_out, tool_call, tool_result} events — but the
Flow page JS was never connecting to it.

Also fix log-stream fallback to match actual OpenClaw outbound pattern: 'sendMessage ok'.

* fix: add /api/flow-events SSE endpoint (no auth) + connect Flow tab to it

- Added /api/flow-events to OSS dashboard — tails gateway.log + active session
  JSONL, emits typed {msg_in, msg_out, tool_call, tool_result} events
- No authentication required (OSS has no signup/token)
- Flow tab JS now connects to this endpoint directly instead of trying to
  pattern-match the unrelated log stream

---------

Co-authored-by: vivekchand <vivek@clawmetry.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct token field names in brain data + gateway/channel sync (v0.11.96) (#80)

* fix: correct OpenClaw field names in brain data + add gateway/channel sync

Brain data (_build_brain_data):
- Fix token field names: inputTokens→input, outputTokens→output,
  cacheReadInputTokens→cacheRead, cacheCreationInputTokens→cacheWrite
- Use usage.cost.total for accurate cost (was estimating from wrong token counts)
- Fix avg_response_ms: compute from user→assistant timestamp delta (durationMs not stored)
- Track prev_user_ts per session file for accurate duration

Channel data (_build_channel_data):
- Rewrite to parse ~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log (plain-text format)
- Old code looked for 'messageChannel=' in JSON logs (never matches OpenClaw format)
- Now correctly counts sendMessage (outgoing) and inbound events per channel

Gateway data (_build_gateway_data):
- New function: parses gateway.log for routing events
- Added 'gateway' key to system snapshot payload

Bumps to v0.11.96

* fix: sync.py gateway + channel data improvements (v0.11.97)

- _build_gateway_data(): rewrite to parse ~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log
  (plain text) instead of JSON log — was looking for 'embedded run start:'
  which never exists; now correctly counts 100+ messages/day
  Route format: {from:channel, to:user, session:msg_id, type, status}

- _build_channel_data(): parse session JSONL files for inbound message count
  (gateway.log only has outbound sendMessage lines; inbound comes from sessions)

- version bump 0.11.96 → 0.11.97

---------

Co-authored-by: vivekchand <vivek@clawmetry.com>

- version bump 0.11.96 → 0.11.97

commit.message: fix: workflow YAML syntax - use shell:python for multiline steps

feat: terminal-first connect flow, E2E key UX, per-node key scoping, auto-sync on account switch, colored CLI output, syncing banner

- clawmetry connect: email OTP sign-up/sign-in (no API key required)
- E2E key prompt on first connect; auto-generate or paste own key
- Per-node localStorage key scoping (cm-enc-key-{node_id})
- Unlock modal on brain decrypt failure (clears bad key + reloads)
- Setup session token: browser auto-receives enc key on first open
- Show masked encryption key when reusing saved key on reconnect
- Terminal ANSI colors: red/grey theme, box drawing, lobster header
- First sync detects account switch via api_key_id hash in state
- Syncing banner in dashboard: shows when session_count=0 or <3min old
- Node version badges + one-click update from fleet cards
- clawmetry connect opens browser after Press Enter pause
- Auto-IP detection via UDP socket trick
- Brain decrypt fail: clear key + reload to show unlock modal

Version: 0.11.94
fix: generic OpenClaw dir search in Docker mounts (os.walk, no hardcoded paths)
fix: Docker detection checks .openclaw subdir in /data mount (Hostinger compat)
fix: robust connect flow (root systemd, pkill, auto-install service, crash recovery, background fallback)
feat: auto-detect OpenClaw in Docker, mirror container data to host for sync
fix: add more session path candidates, OPENCLAW_HOME env, verbose path logging
ci: add cross-platform sync daemon tests (Linux/macOS/Windows) + BrowserStack E2E
fix: mkdir ~/.clawmetry before logging init, stop old daemon on connect, clear sync state
fix: clawmetry connect stops old daemon, clears sync state, restarts with new credentials
fix: full synchronous initial sync on first run - heartbeat + memory + sessions + logs + crons
fix: send heartbeat immediately on daemon startup for instant fleet visibility
fix: sync all workspace files (SOUL/IDENTITY/USER/AGENTS/TOOLS/HEARTBEAT), encrypt memory, new /ingest/memory endpoint
fix: escHtml handles undefined/null safely, prevents Brain tab crash
fix: brain renderBrainStream handles timestamp/ts/time fields for cloud events

fix: reduce sync batch size from 50 to 10 to prevent Turso payload size rejection

Encrypted blobs at batch_size=50 were ~128KB, exceeding Turso HTTP pipeline limits.
Reducing to 10 events per blob (~25KB) ensures reliable writes.

fix: sync daemon extracts token usage + cost from session events, uses last model seen

- Scans all message events for usage.totalTokens and usage.cost.total
- Sums across entire session for accurate totals
- Uses last model seen in messages (fixes sonnet vs opus mismatch)
- Reads label from session events for better display names
chore: bump to 0.11.29
feat(0.11.28): sync session labels from sessions.json; handle raw JSONL message events in ingest; fix display names showing as UUIDs
fix: CLOUD_MODE guards for startLogStream + startHealthStream; prevent 429 reconnect loop
fix: CLOUD_MODE guards show empty states (Memory/Logs), loadSessions handles errors gracefully
chore: bump version to 0.11.26

feat: TursoDataProvider for cloud + CLOUD_MODE guards in loadBrainPage/loadMemory

- Add clawmetry/providers/turso.py: TursoDataProvider implementing ClawMetryDataProvider
  Reads sessions/events from Turso via HTTP pipeline API. owner_hash derived from
  CLAWMETRY_TOKEN env var. Activated via CLAWMETRY_PROVIDER=turso.
- Register TursoDataProvider in providers/__init__.py (graceful fallback if unavailable)
- Add window.CLOUD_MODE guards to loadBrainPage() and loadMemory() so cloud overlay
  no longer needs JS fetch interceptors for these endpoints
fix: update author email to vivek@clawmetry.com in package metadata
fix: Brain tab always shows date prefix (Today/Mar 1); fix author email to clawmetry.com
fix: author email openclaw.dev -> clawmetry.com
fix: restore full README (screenshots, channels, badges) + fix broken CLOUD_TESTING link
docs: add badges (PyPI downloads, stars, version) + expand README
fix: Brain tab — show date+time for non-today events; render JSON/markdown in detail column
ci: GH Actions pins Dockerfile version only — Dhriti cron deploys (SSH not reachable from GH runners)
ci: deploy via SSH to Dhriti instead of gcloud from runner (fixes flaky Cloud Build)

perf: optimize sync_session_metadata to read first/last lines only

Large session files (20MB+) were causing urllib timeout.
Now reads first 20 lines for metadata + last 4KB for updated_at.
Avoids full file scan for sessions with thousands of messages.

fix: sync_session_metadata reads JSONL files directly (HTTP API returns HTML)

- OpenClaw HTTP /api/sessions returns SPA HTML, not JSON
- Read from ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/*.jsonl directly
- Extract model from model_change events, timestamps from all events
- Batch upsert up to 100 sessions per sync cycle

feat: add session metadata sync to sessions table (v0.11.23)

- Add sync_session_metadata() that reads sessions from OpenClaw API
- POST session rows to /ingest/sessions endpoint
- Call in run_daemon() loop alongside existing sync functions
- Fixes: sessions count showing 0 in cloud dashboard

fix: Brain tab sort by full ISO timestamp — newest events always at top

- Client-side: sort by new Date(ev.time).getTime() descending in both
  loadBrainPage and renderBrainStream (fixes HH:MM:SS string comparison)
- Server-side: ISO string sort is lexicographically correct across dates
- CONTEXT events (session start) now correctly sink to bottom as oldest

feat: Brain tab type filter pills + bump v0.11.21

Squash-merges PR #64 (feat/brain-system-context):
- CONTEXT events (📋): shows workspace files loaded into session
- RESULT events (📎): shows tool execution outputs
- New type filter pills for Brain tab
- Full audit chain visibility

bump: v0.11.21
fix: replace broken BrowserStack badge URL with shields.io static badge

ci: auto-deploy cloud on every OSS main push

- Pins clawmetry version in cloud Dockerfile to match OSS
- Triggers gcloud run deploy after each OSS dashboard.py change
- Cloud always runs the same version as the latest OSS release
- Workflow also manually triggerable via workflow_dispatch

fix: Flow tab SVG height fills viewport instead of scaling to full width

- #flow-svg: height: calc(100vh - 155px) with min-height: 400px
- Prevents oversized/zoomed-in appearance on wide screens
- Both dark and light theme blocks patched
fix: add cryptography as required dep — no more pip install cryptography error on connect

fix: remove padding from Flow tab, compact layout (#65)

Co-authored-by: vivekchand <vivek@clawmetry.com>

fix: launchctl bootstrap + remove em dashes from connect output (#66)

* fix: use launchctl bootstrap on macOS (no more I/O error warning)

- Switch from deprecated 'launchctl load -w' to 'launchctl bootstrap gui/UID'
- Suppress stderr so warning noise is gone
- Fix connect URL: clawmetry.com/connect (not app.clawmetry.com)
- Remove em dashes from user-facing terminal output

* fix: simplify connect output for non-technical users

- Friendly language throughout (no jargon)
- Encryption key shown simply with a one-liner note
- Daemon start just says 'Running in the background'
- Final 'All done! Open app.clawmetry.com' message
- launchctl bootstrap instead of deprecated load -w (no I/O error)

---------

commit.message:
fix: send X-Node-Id header in all /api/ingest calls (#63)

The cloud /api/ingest requires X-Node-Id header. The _post() helper
now auto-extracts node_id from the payload and sets it as a header.
Fixes cron and memory sync 400 errors.

Bumps to v0.11.16

Co-authored-by: vivekchand <vivek@clawmetry.com>

commit.message:
fix: Full Logs expanded items persist across auto-refresh (#62)

* fix: Full Logs expanded items persist across auto-refresh

Expanded event bodies in the task modal Full Logs tab now stay
open when auto-refresh re-renders the content. Previously, the
4-second refresh would innerHTML-replace everything, collapsing
any expanded items.

Uses _expandedEvts dict to track open state by index.

* fix: expanded log entries no longer collapse on auto-refresh

The Full Logs tab in the sub-agent modal auto-refreshes every 4s,
which re-rendered the entire list and destroyed the open/closed state
of expanded entries. Now tracks which entries are expanded in
_expandedEvts{} and restores their state after each re-render.

Click to expand, stays expanded until you click again. No more
chasing disappearing content.

Bumps to v0.11.15

---------

Co-authored-by: vivekchand <vivek@clawmetry.com>

author Vivek Chand <vivekchand19@gmail.com> 1772413194 +0100
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1772413194 +0100

fix: Full Logs expanded items persist across auto-refresh (#62)

* fix: Full Logs expanded items persist across auto-refresh

Expanded event bodies in the task modal Full Logs tab now stay
open when auto-refresh re-renders the content. Previously, the
4-second refresh would innerHTML-replace everything, collapsing
any expanded items.

Uses _expandedEvts dict to track open state by index.

* fix: expanded log entries no longer collapse on auto-refresh

The Full Logs tab in the sub-agent modal auto-refreshes every 4s,
which re-rendered the entire list and destroyed the open/closed state
of expanded entries. Now tracks which entries are expanded in
_expandedEvts{} and restores their state after each re-render.

Click to expand, stays expanded until you click again. No more
chasing disappearing content.

Bumps to v0.11.15

---------

Co-authored-by: vivekchand <vivek@clawmetry.com>

commit.message:
feat: real-time log streaming to cloud (#60)

- start_log_streamer() runs background thread tailing local logs
- POSTs lines to /ingest/stream every 2s (or 50-line batches)
- Auto-rotates to latest log file daily
- Powers live Flow tab + Logs tab on cloud dashboard

Bumps to v0.11.14

Co-authored-by: vivekchand <vivek@clawmetry.com>

commit.message:
feat: sync memory files to cloud (MEMORY.md + memory/*.md) (#59)

- sync_memory() pushes memory_state (file list) and memory_content (file contents)
- Only syncs changed files (MD5 hash tracking in sync-state.json)
- detect_paths() now also finds workspace directory
- Daemon loop includes memory sync alongside sessions + logs
- Content capped at 100KB per file

Bumps to v0.11.13

Co-authored-by: vivekchand <vivek@clawmetry.com>

commit.message:
feat: support ?token=XXX URL parameter for gateway auto-config (#58)

* feat: support ?token=XXX URL parameter for gateway auto-config

- checkGwConfig() now reads ?token= from URL on page load
- Auto-stores token in localStorage and POSTs to /api/gw/config
- Strips token from URL bar via history.replaceState (security)
- Reloads page after successful connect
- Enables bookmarkable URLs: http://localhost:8900/?token=YOUR_TOKEN
- Also enables cloud redirect flow: app.clawmetry.com/auth?token=cm_xxx

Bumps to v0.11.12

* feat: add /auth?token=XXX route for one-click authentication

- GET /auth?token=YOUR_TOKEN stores token in localStorage and redirects to /
- Works for OSS gateway tokens and cloud cm_ keys
- Enables: http://localhost:8900/auth?token=abc123
- Enables: app.clawmetry.com/auth?token=cm_xxx
- Clean error page if token is missing

---------

Co-authored-by: vivekchand <vivek@clawmetry.com>

commit.message: chore: bump to v0.11.11

feat: Phase 5 — clawmetry connect/disconnect/status + sync daemon (#57)

* feat: Phase 5 — clawmetry connect/disconnect/status + sync daemon

Closes #48

New commands:
  clawmetry connect [--key cm_xxx]   Validate key, save config, register daemon
  clawmetry disconnect               Stop daemon, remove key/config
  clawmetry status                   Show cloud + daemon status

New file: clawmetry/sync.py
  - run_daemon(): polls every 15s, streams to ingest.clawmetry.com
  - sync_sessions(): tails *.jsonl files incrementally (cursor-based)
  - sync_logs(): tails openclaw-*.log files by byte offset
  - send_heartbeat(): pings ingest every 60s with node metadata
  - detect_paths(): mirrors dashboard.py path detection (incl. Docker)
  - Persists cursor state to ~/.clawmetry/sync-state.json
  - Logs to ~/.clawmetry/sync.log

Updated: clawmetry/cli.py
  - Intercepts connect/disconnect/status before delegating to dashboard
  - launchd registration on macOS (~/Library/LaunchAgents/)
  - systemd --user registration on Linux
  - subprocess fallback for Windows
  - Graceful degradation if ingest unreachable (saves config anyway)

Config: ~/.clawmetry/config.json (chmod 600)
  { api_key, node_id, platform, connected_at }

User flow:
  pip install clawmetry
  clawmetry start --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8900   # unchanged
  clawmetry connect                              # new
  # → Streaming to app.clawmetry.com as Mac-Diya

* feat: E2E encryption + full content sync (not just metadata)

- sync.py: send full JSONL events + full log lines (not stripped metadata)
- E2E encryption with AES-256-GCM before any data leaves the machine
- generate_encryption_key(): 256-bit key via secrets.token_bytes(32)
- encrypt_payload(): base64url(nonce || ciphertext), nonce per batch
- decrypt_payload(): for use by web/iOS/Mac clients
- Server receives opaque encrypted blobs — can never read plaintext
- connect: auto-generates encryption key, displays it prominently
- status: shows masked key by default, --show-key to reveal
- Encrypted POSTs include encrypted=true flag so server can route correctly
- cryptography package required (pip install cryptography)

---------

Co-authored-by: vivekchand <vivek@clawmetry.com>

commit.message: chore: bump to v0.11.10

feat: Phase 4 — Flask Blueprint split (18 route groups) (#56)

Routes reorganized from monolithic @app.route into 18 Flask Blueprints:

  bp_auth       — /api/auth/check, /
  bp_gateway    — /api/gw/*
  bp_overview   — /api/overview, /api/channels, /api/main-activity, /api/timeline
  bp_sessions   — /api/sessions, /api/transcripts, /api/transcript/*, /api/subagents
  bp_crons      — /api/crons, /api/cron/*
  bp_logs       — /api/logs, /api/logs-stream
  bp_brain      — /api/brain-history
  bp_memory     — /api/memory-files, /api/file
  bp_usage      — /api/usage, /api/usage/export
  bp_budget     — /api/budget/*
  bp_alerts     — /api/alerts/*
  bp_history    — /api/history/*
  bp_fleet      — /fleet, /api/nodes/*
  bp_channels   — /api/channel/* (21 channels)
  bp_health     — /api/health, /api/health-stream, /api/system-health, /api/heatmap
  bp_otel       — /v1/metrics, /v1/traces, /api/otel-status
  bp_components — /api/component/*
  bp_config     — /api/llmfit, /api/cost-optimizer, /api/cost-optimization, /api/automation-analysis

All 88 routes now blueprint-namespaced. Handler code stays in dashboard.py
(file extraction is Phase 4.5 / Phase 5 territory). Zero breaking changes.

Closes #47

Co-authored-by: vivekchand <vivek@clawmetry.com>

commit.message: chore: bump to v0.11.9

fix: brain tab now shows user prompts, thinking blocks and agent responses

- Add USER type: extracts user message text from role=user entries
- Add THINK type: extracts thinking/reasoning block content
- Add AGENT type: extracts assistant text response content
- Add icons: 💬 USER, 🧠 THINK, 🤖 AGENT in _brainTypeIcons
- Previously brain tab only showed tool calls (SEARCH, EXEC etc)
- Now shows the full picture: what user said → what AI thought → what it did
chore: bump to v0.11.8

feat: Phase 3 — DataProvider ABC + LocalDataProvider + provider registry (#54)

* feat: Phase 3 — DataProvider ABC + LocalDataProvider + provider registry

- clawmetry/providers/base.py: ClawMetryDataProvider ABC with dataclasses
  (Session, Event, LogEntry, MemoryFile, MetricPoint) and abstract methods
  for sessions, events, logs, memory, crons
- clawmetry/providers/local.py: LocalDataProvider — reads ~/.openclaw files
  exactly matching existing dashboard.py behavior
- clawmetry/providers/__init__.py: provider registry with init_providers(),
  entry_points discovery for 3rd-party providers (e.g. TursoDataProvider)
- dashboard.py: add _init_data_provider() helper + call it after path detection

Cloud implements TursoDataProvider in clawmetry-cloud via entry_points:
  [project.entry-points."clawmetry.providers"]
  turso = "clawmetry_cloud.providers:TursoDataProvider"

Closes #46

* chore: add Phase 3 screenshots

* feat: Phase 3 — DataProvider ABC + LocalDataProvider + provider registry

- Add clawmetry/providers/base.py: ClawMetryDataProvider ABC with dataclasses
  (Session, Event, LogEntry, MemoryFile, MetricPoint)
- Add clawmetry/providers/local.py: LocalDataProvider — mirrors existing file
  reads (sessions.json, JSONL events, log files, memory files, crons)
- Add clawmetry/providers/__init__.py: provider registry with init_providers(),
  entry_points discovery for 3rd-party providers (e.g. TursoDataProvider)
- dashboard.py: add _init_data_provider() + wire into path setup startup
- CLAWMETRY_PROVIDER env var selects provider (default: local)
- All existing routes unchanged — provider runs alongside, not replacing
- Closes #46

---------

Co-authored-by: vivekchand <vivek@clawmetry.com>

- dashboard.py: add _init_data_provider() helper + call it after path detection

Cloud implements TursoDataProvider in clawmetry-cloud via entry_points:
  [project.entry-points."clawmetry.providers"]
  turso = "clawmetry_cloud.providers:TursoDataProvider"

Closes #46

* chore: add Phase 3 screenshots

* feat: Phase 3 — DataProvider ABC + LocalDataProvider + provider registry

- Add clawmetry/providers/base.py: ClawMetryDataProvider ABC with dataclasses
  (Session, Event, LogEntry, MemoryFile, MetricPoint)
- Add clawmetry/providers/local.py: LocalDataProvider — mirrors existing file
  reads (sessions.json, JSONL events, log files, memory files, crons)
- Add clawmetry/providers/__init__.py: provider registry with init_providers(),
  entry_points discovery for 3rd-party providers (e.g. TursoDataProvider)
- dashboard.py: add _init_data_provider() + wire into path setup startup
- CLAWMETRY_PROVIDER env var selects provider (default: local)
- All existing routes unchanged — provider runs alongside, not replacing
- Closes #46

---------

Co-authored-by: vivekchand <vivek@clawmetry.com>

commit.message: chore: bump to v0.11.7

feat: Phase 2 — extensions.py plugin system + ClawMetryConfig dataclass (#51)

* feat: Phase 2 — extensions.py plugin system + ClawMetryConfig dataclass

- Add clawmetry/extensions.py: register/emit/load_plugins for external hooks
  - Thread-safe handler registry
  - Auto-discovers plugins via importlib.metadata entry_points
  - Exceptions in handlers never propagate to dashboard
- Add clawmetry/config.py: ClawMetryConfig dataclass (migration bridge for Phase 3)
- dashboard.py: add extension import block (safe no-op if clawmetry not installed)
- dashboard.py: add 7 emit() calls at key lifecycle points:
  auth.check, session.snapshot, usage.compiled, brain.event,
  log.ingested, cron.run, fleet.node_register
- All emit() calls wrapped in try/except — zero behavior change if extensions absent
- Closes #45

* fix: break circular import between dashboard.py and clawmetry package on Windows

- clawmetry/__init__.py: no longer imports from dashboard (caused circular
  import → Windows stdout wrap crash). Now reads __version__ via regex and
  calls dashboard.main() lazily inside main()
- dashboard.py: fix indentation of win32 stdout/stderr TextIOWrapper wrap
  (try/except block was malformed after prior edit)

Fixes Windows CI failure: ValueError: I/O operation on closed file

* chore: add Phase 2 Playwright screenshots

---------

Co-authored-by: vivekchand <vivek@clawmetry.com>

readme_html:
🦞 ClawMetry

See your agent think. Real-time observability for OpenClaw AI agents.

One command. Zero config. Auto-detects everything.

pip install clawmetry && clawmetry

Opens at http://localhost:8900 and you're done.

What You Get

  • Flow — Live animated diagram showing messages flowing through channels, brain, tools, and back
  • Overview — Health checks, activity heatmap, session counts, model info
  • Usage — Token and cost tracking with daily/weekly/monthly breakdowns
  • Sessions — Active agent sessions with model, tokens, last activity
  • Crons — Scheduled jobs with status, next run, duration
  • Logs — Color-coded real-time log streaming
  • Memory — Browse SOUL.md, MEMORY.md, AGENTS.md, daily notes
  • Transcripts — Chat-bubble UI for reading session histories

Screenshots

Flow Overview Sub-Agent
Summary Crons Memory

Install

pip (recommended):

pip install clawmetry
clawmetry

One-liner:

curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vivekchand/clawmetry/main/install.sh | bash

From source:

git clone https://github.com/vivekchand/clawmetry.git
cd clawmetry && pip install flask && python3 dashboard.py

Configuration

Most people don't need any config. ClawMetry auto-detects your workspace, logs, sessions, and crons.

If you do need to customize:

clawmetry --port 9000              # Custom port (default: 8900)
clawmetry --host 127.0.0.1         # Bind to localhost only
clawmetry --workspace ~/mybot      # Custom workspace path
clawmetry --name "Alice"           # Your name in Flow visualization

All options: clawmetry --help

Supported Channels

ClawMetry shows live activity for every OpenClaw channel you have configured. Only channels that are actually set up in your openclaw.json appear in the Flow diagram — unconfigured ones are automatically hidden.

Click any channel node in the Flow to see a live chat bubble view with incoming/outgoing message counts.

Channel Status Live Popup Notes
📱 Telegram ✅ Full ✅ Messages, stats, 10s refresh
💬 iMessage ✅ Full ✅ Reads ~/Library/Messages/chat.db directly
💚 WhatsApp ✅ Full ✅ Via WhatsApp Web (Baileys)
🔵 Signal ✅ Full ✅ Via signal-cli
🟣 Discord ✅ Full ✅ Guild + channel detection
🟪 Slack ✅ Full ✅ Workspace + channel detection
🌐 Webchat ✅ Full ✅ Built-in web UI sessions
📡 IRC ✅ Full ✅ Terminal-style bubble UI
🍏 BlueBubbles ✅ Full ✅ iMessage via BlueBubbles REST API
🔵 Google Chat ✅ Full ✅ Via Chat API webhooks
🟣 MS Teams ✅ Full ✅ Via Teams bot plugin
🔷 Mattermost ✅ Full ✅ Self-hosted team chat
🟩 Matrix ✅ Full ✅ Decentralized, E2EE support
🟢 LINE ✅ Full ✅ LINE Messaging API
⚡ Nostr ✅ Full ✅ Decentralized NIP-04 DMs
🟣 Twitch ✅ Full ✅ Chat via IRC connection
🔷 Feishu/Lark ✅ Full ✅ WebSocket event subscription
🔵 Zalo ✅ Full ✅ Zalo Bot API

Auto-detection: ClawMetry reads your ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json and only renders the channels you've actually configured. No manual setup required.

Requirements

  • Python 3.8+
  • Flask (installed automatically via pip)
  • OpenClaw running on the same machine
  • Linux or macOS

Cloud Deployment

See the Cloud Testing Guide for SSH tunnels, reverse proxy, and Docker.

Testing

This project is tested with BrowserStack.

License

MIT
